title: Lotodes pinnatum
intro: Lotodes pinnatum, also known as Tinospora cordifolia or Guduchi, is a tropical vine native to the Indian subcontinent, belonging to the family Menispermaceae. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, which may help in reducing fever, and improving digestion and respiratory health. The bioactive constituents of Lotodes pinnomaturn include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Lotodes pinnatum is commonly used in herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and powder, often combined with other herbs in traditional Ayurvedic formulations to enhance its therapeutic effects.
taxonomy: Lotodes pinnatum is a type of algae found in the ocean. It belongs to the family Chordariaceae, which is part of the class Phaeophyceae. The class Phaeophyceae is a group of brown algae, and the family Chordariaceae is a specific group within this class. Lotodes pinnatum's classification provides insight into its evolutionary history and its relationships with other algae species.
